Definition: Harmothoe areolata genome assembly, chromosome: 3
Description: Harmothoe areolata genome assembly, chromosome: 3
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Annelida; Polychaeta; Errantia; Phyllodocida; Polynoidae; Harmothoe
Assembly: The assembly of Harmothoe areolata (wpHarAreo1.1) is based on 41X PacBio data and Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 853 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 40.89 Mb (with the largest being 1.79 Mb). Additionally, 591 regions totaling 125.49 Mb (with the largest being 6.41 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 42 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 72.56 Mb (with the largest being 6.43 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Definition: Clytie syriaca genome assembly, chromosome: 22
Description: Clytie syriaca genome assembly, chromosome: 22
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Erebidae; Erebinae; Clytie
Assembly: The assembly ilClySyri1.hap2.1 is based on 61x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Definition: Vulgichneumon bimaculatus genome assembly, chromosome: 3
Description: Vulgichneumon bimaculatus genome assembly, chromosome: 3
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Hymenoptera; Apocrita; Ichneumonoidea; Ichneumonidae; Ichneumoninae; Vulgichneumon
Assembly: The assembly iyVulBima1.1 is based on 147x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (11.6Mb - 17.4Mb) and chromosome 12 (10.3Mb - 15.6Mb) are unknown. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.
